summary: PR 2385 decompiled and typed several pltrick/player-stat paths, adding implementations for pltrick functions, tightening related prototypes, and refining fighter/player structs to expose bitfields and a float field. The bot report records new matches for fn_80037F00 and pl_80038628 plus near-complete improvements for pl_80037C60, pl_80038144, and pl_800384DC.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: added includes, static helper declaration, pl_803BCE70 table, implementations for pl_80037C60, fn_80037F00, pl_80038144, pl_800384DC, and pl_80038628, plus a local inline wrapper for pl_80037BC0.
Header updated from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incomplete prototypes to typed signatures for recovered pltrick functions.
Type recovery for fighter-related bitfields: Struct2074 x2084 became a union with byte bitfields, and ft_800898B4_t bitfields changed from u32 to u8.
Refined pl_804D6470_t layout by exposing float x14 after shrinking padding.
Adjusted several plbonuslib function signatures to carry an additional int argument and removed one PAD_STACK in pl_8003ED0C.
Synchronized plbonuslib prototypes with body signature changes.
Changed unused UNK_T parameters to int for pl_80040FBC and pl_800410F4.
Synchronized pl_040D prototypes with int parameter changes.

what_changed: Implemented fn_80037F00 as a static helper in pltrick.c, updating fighter damage event state, percent snapshot, magnify/status flags, knockback-threshold behavior via pl_804D6470_t.x14, victim checks through ft_80089914, and x10_b7 marking.
Implemented pl_80038628, including an HSD_ASSERT on kind range 1..0x10, lookup through new pl_803BCE70[16], and ftData_80085FD4(...)->x8 gating.

Added partial or near-matching implementations for pl_80037C60, pl_80038144, and pl_800384DC involving attack/hit stat counters, Struct2070 event decoding, team/stale-move calls, and bonuslib/pl_040D calls.
Added a static inline pl_80037BC0_inline wrapper that forwards to pl_80037BC0 and is used from the new pltrick implementations.
Updated pltrick.h prototypes, removing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for recovered signatures and typing pl_80038144 arguments including ft_800898B4_t* and u16 attack_instance.
Updated related plbonuslib and pl_040D function signatures and headers to pass player slot/team-like int arguments used by the new pltrick call sites.
Recovered field access in ft/types.h and pl/types.h by exposing bitfields and pl_804D6470_t.x14 instead of leaving opaque words or padding.
Bot report recorded +756 matched-code bytes, +8 matched-data bytes, 3 new matches, and 4 unmatched-item improvements.

smart_moves.text: Body/header synchronization was done alongside decompilation: pltrick.h, plbonuslib.h, and pl_040D.h were updated to match recovered function bodies and call sites.
The PR used recovered struct fields and bitfields to express event flags and table data, enabling readable accesses such as fp->x2074.x2084_b7, ev_data->x10_b7, and table->x14.
The PR kept fn_80037F00 source-local by declaring it static after replacing the placeholder, avoiding a public header declaration for a helper not shown as externally needed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pltrick.h removed fn_80037F00 public prototype

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering a function that needs additional caller context, update the callee body and owning header together, then adjust new call sites to pass the recovered argument list.
Typed bitfields can make decompiled fighter event/status code more direct than raw word access when individual flag bits are used throughout the recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pltrick.c x2084_b7 and x10/x11 flag accesses

decomp_lessons.lesson: Opaque padding can be narrowed when a recovered function proves a specific field access, as with pl_804D6470_t.x14 used for a knockback threshold in fn_80037F00.

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style function names were retained for unresolved player/pltrick functions, while local variable names describe roles where apparent, such as attacker_fp, victim_fp, ev_data, attack_instance, source_ply, stale_moves, and attacked_from_behind.
Recovered fields retained offset-style names where semantics were not fully proven, such as x2084_b7, x10_b7, x11_b4, and x14.
Function prototype parameters moved away from UNK_T/UNK_PARAMS when body and call-site evidence supported concrete int, s32, u16, pointer, and Fighter_GObj/HSD_GObj types.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in fn_80037F00 and pl_80038144/pl_800384DC, while removing PAD_STACK(8) from pl_8003ED0C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR evidence shows tactic use but does not include author explanation; bot output confirms function-level matching/progress, not the exact necessity of each PAD_STACK.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a volatile local in pl_80037C60 for prev2070_word with an inline source comment noting stack misalignment and uncertainty about an inline.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_80037C60 volatile s32 prev2070_word and comment
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is explicitly marked uncertain in-source and should not be generalized as preferred style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added a local static inline wrapper around pl_80037BC0 and called the wrapper from newly recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_80038144 and pl_800384DC call sites
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Evidence shows the wrapper exists; no review or author note explains why it was required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recovered table/data participation for pl_803BCE70 and .sdata2/.data progress in pltrick.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The bot report supports data-section progress; ownership rationale is not described in PR text.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 61.83% (+0.02%, +756 bytes), matched data 37.87% (+0.00%, +8 bytes), 3 new matches, and 4 improvements in unmatched pltrick items.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching pl_80037C60 stack misalignment or volatile prev2070_word
Find later PRs completing pl_80038144 or pl_800384DC after 96% progress
Search pl_803BCE70 data ownership and symbol metadata in pltrick
Search ft_800898B4_t bitfield changes and x10_b7/x11_b* usage
Search pl_804D6470_t.x14 naming or semantic recovery
Search PAD_STACK uses in src/melee/pl/pltrick.c after PR 2385

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
Automated bot report provides concrete match/progress evidence, including exact matched bytes and per-item percentages.
Diff supports changed files, signatures, struct field recovery, and tactic usage, but does not prove author intent for PAD_STACK, volatile, inline wrapper, or data ownership choices.
Some functions were improved but not fully matched according to the bot report: pl_80038144 at 96.90%, pl_80037C60 at 99.98%, and pl_800384DC at 96.77%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed bitfields helped pltrick event-flag decompilation
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2385 changed ft/types.h to expose byte-sized bitfields in Struct2074 and ft_800898B4_t, then used those fields in pltrick implementations such as fp->x2074.x2084_b7 and ev_data->x10_b7. This is a concrete instance supporting the existing typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered pltrick signatures required synchronized caller and header updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2385 updated prototypes in pltrick.h, plbonuslib.h, and pl_040D.h while changing bodies and pltrick call sites to use concrete int/s32/u16/pointer parameters instead of UNK_T/UNK_PARAMS placeholders.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: header and body signature changes

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2385 pltrick match/progress facts
source_update_candidates.text: For src/melee/pl/pltrick.c, PR 2385 added implementations/progress for pl_80037C60, fn_80037F00, pl_80038144, pl_800384DC, and pl_80038628. The bot report recorded fn_80037F00 and pl_80038628 as new 100% matches, plus pl_80038144 at 96.90%, pl_80037C60 at 99.98%, and pl_800384DC at 96.77%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-specific historical progress fact; should be curated rather than promoted directly by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: pltrick related type recovery in PR 2385
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2385 exposed Struct2074.x2084 bitfields, changed ft_800898B4_t bitfields to u8, and added pl_804D6470_t.x14 as a float used by fn_80037F00.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful type-history fact for future work on fighter damage event data and pl table layout.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that PAD_STACK or volatile locals are generally acceptable from this PR; the only explicit source comment says pl_80037C60 stack is still misaligned and the inline hypothesis is uncertain.
Do not treat pl_80038144, pl_80037C60, or pl_800384DC as fully matched based on this slice; the bot report lists them as improvements below 100%.
Do not infer semantic names for x10_b*, x11_b*, x2084_b*, or x14 beyond the offset-style names shown in the diff.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; the loaded slice contains no human review comments.
